entries.statement: covid.origins: the 155 December-2019 cases with location data had a median distance of 4.28 km to the Huanan market (P<0.001) against 16.11 km for age-matched controls

canonical.statement: covid.origins: the 155 December-2019 cases with location data had a median distance of 4.28 km to the Huanan market (P<0.001) against 16.11 km for age-matched controls

entries.statement: covid.origins: the market spatial association survived sensitivity analysis: up to 38 (24%) of the closest cases could be removed before losing significance on the center-point test, 98 (63%) on the median test

canonical.statement: covid.origins: the market spatial association survived sensitivity analysis: up to 38 (24%) of the closest cases could be removed before losing significance on the center-point test, 98 (63%) on the median test

entries.statement: covid.origins: of 923 environmental samples from the market, 74 (8.0%) were positive for SARS-CoV-2, and 87.5% (56/64) of inside-market positives came from the wildlife-selling West Zone; all 457 animal samples were negative

canonical.statement: covid.origins: of 923 environmental samples from the market, 74 (8.0%) were positive for SARS-CoV-2, and 87.5% (56/64) of inside-market positives came from the wildlife-selling West Zone; all 457 animal samples were negative

entries.statement: covid.origins: all five positive environmental samples from one wildlife stall contained raccoon-dog, bamboo-rat, and civet DNA exceeding human DNA, alongside related animal viruses (an observation of wildlife DNA, not of infected animals)

canonical.statement: covid.origins: all five positive environmental samples from one wildlife stall contained raccoon-dog, bamboo-rat, and civet DNA exceeding human DNA, alongside related animal viruses (an observation of wildlife DNA, not of infected animals)

entries.statement: covid.origins: both SARS-CoV-2 lineages A and B are linked to the market: lineage B in market cases and samples, lineage A in a glove sample and in early cases within 2 km of the market (an observation of lineage location, not of two spillovers)

canonical.statement: covid.origins: both SARS-CoV-2 lineages A and B are linked to the market: lineage B in market cases and samples, lineage A in a glove sample and in early cases within 2 km of the market (an observation of lineage location, not of two spillovers)

entries.statement: covid.origins: the SARS-CoV-2 polybasic furin cleavage site was created by a precise 12-nucleotide insertion (PRRA) at the S1/S2 junction, and it is the only sarbecovirus of more than 800 known to carry one

canonical.statement: covid.origins: the SARS-CoV-2 polybasic furin cleavage site was created by a precise 12-nucleotide insertion (PRRA) at the S1/S2 junction, and it is the only sarbecovirus of more than 800 known to carry one

entries.statement: covid.origins: the arginine codon CGG occurs at only 3-5% frequency in sarbecoviruses, so the CGG-CGG pair in the furin insertion is rare, and both codons are over 99.8% conserved across more than 2.3 million genomes

canonical.statement: covid.origins: the arginine codon CGG occurs at only 3-5% frequency in sarbecoviruses, so the CGG-CGG pair in the furin insertion is rare, and both codons are over 99.8% conserved across more than 2.3 million genomes

entries.statement: covid.origins: the 2018 DEFUSE proposal to DARPA, by researchers including WIV collaborators, described inserting human-specific furin cleavage sites into bat SARS-related coronaviruses; it was not funded

canonical.statement: covid.origins: the 2018 DEFUSE proposal to DARPA, by researchers including WIV collaborators, described inserting human-specific furin cleavage sites into bat SARS-related coronaviruses; it was not funded

entries.statement: covid.origins: no live raccoon dogs, civets, or porcupines were tested for SARS-CoV-2 at the market, the animals having been cleared before sampling began, and the market was sanitized on 1 January 2020 before systematic sampling

canonical.statement: covid.origins: no live raccoon dogs, civets, or porcupines were tested for SARS-CoV-2 at the market, the animals having been cleared before sampling began, and the market was sanitized on 1 January 2020 before systematic sampling

entries.statement: covid.origins: about one million wild mammals on Hubei wildlife farms supplying the market were released, sold, or killed in early 2020 without SARS-CoV-2 testing

canonical.statement: covid.origins: about one million wild mammals on Hubei wildlife farms supplying the market were released, sold, or killed in early 2020 without SARS-CoV-2 testing

entries.statement: covid.origins: that infected animals, not infected humans, deposited the market's viral RNA is argued from the wildlife-DNA samples, and is contested

canonical.statement: covid.origins: that infected animals, not infected humans, deposited the market's viral RNA is argued from the wildlife-DNA samples, and is contested

entries.statement: covid.origins: that the two lineages record two separate zoonotic spillovers is contested: the Pekar Bayes factor fell from ~60 to ~4.3 after three coding errors, and a further correction may reverse it toward a single introduction

canonical.statement: covid.origins: that the two lineages record two separate zoonotic spillovers is contested: the Pekar Bayes factor fell from ~60 to ~4.3 after three coding errors, and a further correction may reverse it toward a single introduction

entries.statement: covid.origins: the furin site is functionally suboptimal and out-of-frame: natural-origin proponents read this as inconsistent with engineering, lab-leak proponents as consistent with an inexperienced engineer

canonical.statement: covid.origins: the furin site is functionally suboptimal and out-of-frame: natural-origin proponents read this as inconsistent with engineering, lab-leak proponents as consistent with an inexperienced engineer

entries.statement: covid.origins: that unlinked cases sat closer to the market than linked cases reveals proximity ascertainment bias, so the clustering may reflect where people looked rather than where the virus started (Debarre and Worobey dispute this via commuting patterns)

canonical.statement: covid.origins: that unlinked cases sat closer to the market than linked cases reveals proximity ascertainment bias, so the clustering may reflect where people looked rather than where the virus started (Debarre and Worobey dispute this via commuting patterns)

entries.statement: covid.origins: a reanalysis found SARS-CoV-2 abundance correlated most with implausible hosts (fish, cow), so the market-wide metagenomic content is uninformative about which animals were infected (Debarre critiques the ignored spatial-temporal structure)

canonical.statement: covid.origins: a reanalysis found SARS-CoV-2 abundance correlated most with implausible hosts (fish, cow), so the market-wide metagenomic content is uninformative about which animals were infected (Debarre critiques the ignored spatial-temporal structure)

entries.statement: covid.origins: that a centroid of early cases must be the origin is unproved and the Monte Carlo null was flawed, though under a corrected implementation the market stays within the 95% confidence region (p=0.89)

canonical.statement: covid.origins: that a centroid of early cases must be the origin is unproved and the Monte Carlo null was flawed, though under a corrected implementation the market stays within the 95% confidence region (p=0.89)

entries.statement: covid.origins: zoonotic spillover at the market is the more likely origin (Peter Miller's conclusion), resting on the shared evidence read under his priors

canonical.statement: covid.origins: zoonotic spillover at the market is the more likely origin (Peter Miller's conclusion), resting on the shared evidence read under his priors

entries.statement: covid.origins: a research-related lab leak is the more likely origin (Rootclaim's conclusion), resting on the same shared evidence read under its priors

canonical.statement: covid.origins: a research-related lab leak is the more likely origin (Rootclaim's conclusion), resting on the same shared evidence read under its priors

entries.statement: covid.origins: Miller's prior: a zoonotic pandemic starts in any given year with about 1% probability (one natural pandemic per century), a historical base-rate judgment

canonical.statement: covid.origins: Miller's prior: a zoonotic pandemic starts in any given year with about 1% probability (one natural pandemic per century), a historical base-rate judgment

entries.statement: covid.origins: Miller's prior: a lab-leak origin at about 1 in 588 (0.0005 that a lab made such a virus in 2019, times 0.05 that it leaked)

canonical.statement: covid.origins: Miller's prior: a lab-leak origin at about 1 in 588 (0.0005 that a lab made such a virus in 2019, times 0.05 that it leaked)

entries.statement: covid.origins: Rootclaim's prior: the market early cluster is only 2x evidence (near-neutral), since markets repeatedly form early COVID clusters regardless of origin, against Miller's ~10,000x

canonical.statement: covid.origins: Rootclaim's prior: the market early cluster is only 2x evidence (near-neutral), since markets repeatedly form early COVID clusters regardless of origin, against Miller's ~10,000x

entries.statement: covid.origins: Rootclaim's prior: a researcher working on a SARS-CoV-2-like virus under BSL-2 has about a 15% annual infection probability, and the WIV was planning gain-of-function work under BSL-2

canonical.statement: covid.origins: Rootclaim's prior: a researcher working on a SARS-CoV-2-like virus under BSL-2 has about a 15% annual infection probability, and the WIV was planning gain-of-function work under BSL-2

entries.statement: covid.origins: six independent Bayesian analyses of the same evidence spanned 23.4 orders of magnitude, from Miller's pro-zoonosis extreme to Rootclaim's pro-lab-leak central estimate, the quantitative form of the crux

canonical.statement: covid.origins: six independent Bayesian analyses of the same evidence spanned 23.4 orders of magnitude, from Miller's pro-zoonosis extreme to Rootclaim's pro-lab-leak central estimate, the quantitative form of the crux

entries.statement: covid.origins: a confident origin verdict performs a settlement the evidence does not carry: with a 23-order spread across analysts, the conclusion is prior-driven, not measured

canonical.statement: covid.origins: a confident origin verdict performs a settlement the evidence does not carry: with a 23-order spread across analysts, the conclusion is prior-driven, not measured

entries.statement: covid.origins: both judges ruled for zoonosis under their own priors (Van Treuren ~1 in 300, Stansifer ~1 in 3000) while treating the Bayesian calculation as secondary to qualitative reasoning: an expert forum judgment, not a measurement

canonical.statement: covid.origins: both judges ruled for zoonosis under their own priors (Van Treuren ~1 in 300, Stansifer ~1 in 3000) while treating the Bayesian calculation as secondary to qualitative reasoning: an expert forum judgment, not a measurement

entries.statement: covid.origins: the conclusion is highly sensitive to the ordering and independence of the evidence: heroic Bayesian analysis multiplies correlated stages (the Multiple Stage Fallacy), and steelmanning one side compresses its range under adversarial averaging

canonical.statement: covid.origins: the conclusion is highly sensitive to the ordering and independence of the evidence: heroic Bayesian analysis multiplies correlated stages (the Multiple Stage Fallacy), and steelmanning one side compresses its range under adversarial averaging

entries.statement: covid.origins (deep): certain live-debate exchanges are attested only in secondary summaries with no written primary and no timestamped transcript in the accessible corpus, so they are recorded as a known coverage gap rather than grounded; examples include the live cage-air-gap and multiple-spillover exchange and the step-by-step space-lizard per-observation derivation; and the endonuclease restriction-map fingerprint circulating around the debate is post-debate literature, not argued in the debate, noted here as out of scope

canonical.statement: covid.origins (deep): certain live-debate exchanges are attested only in secondary summaries with no written primary and no timestamped transcript in the accessible corpus, so they are recorded as a known coverage gap rather than grounded; examples include the live cage-air-gap and multiple-spillover exchange and the step-by-step space-lizard per-observation derivation; and the endonuclease restriction-map fingerprint circulating around the debate is post-debate literature, not argued in the debate, noted here as out of scope

sources.description: Scott Alexander (2024), Astral Codex Ten, 'Practically-A-Book-Review: Rootclaim Lab Leak Debate'
Bloom (2023), Virus Evolution 9(2):vead050 (metagenomic reanalysis); Debarre rebuttal
Crits-Christoph et al. (2024), Cell 187(18):5318-5329 (market wildlife genetic tracing)

Extraction and Evaluation of the SARS-CoV-2 Origins Debate: A Comprehensive Synthesis (2024), the deep argument sets, nested cruxes, and reader weightings of Scott Alexander, Will Van Treuren, and Eric Stansifer over the Rootclaim debate; the deep pass now also incorporates the debate's primary written record (Rootclaim's blog, Miller's Medium, the judges' written decisions, and named post-debate reconstructions)
The Intercept (2021), DEFUSE leaked proposal; US House Oversight letter (2024)
Holmes et al. (2021), Cell 184(19):4848-4856 (FCS suboptimality critical review)
Liu et al. (2023), Nature 618:640-646 (environmental surveillance of the market)
Pekar et al. (2022), Science 377(6609):960-966 (two lineages; erratum 2023)
Romeu (2023), BMC Genomic Data 24:57; Holmes et al. (2021), Cell 184(19):4848 (CGG codons)
Rootclaim Blog (2024), debate results and response to Scott Alexander (Rootclaim's priors)
Segreto & Deigin (2020), BioEssays 42(12):e2000240 (furin cleavage site structure)
Judge Eric Stansifer, final decision (Feb 17 2024): ~1 in 3000 lab leak
Stoyan & Chiu (2024), JRSS-A; Debarre & Worobey arXiv:2403.05859 (centroid critique)
Judge Will Van Treuren, written decision (Feb 2024): ~1 in 300 lab leak
Weissman (2024), JRSS-A 187(3):720 (proximity ascertainment bias); Debarre/Worobey reply
Weissman (2026), Econ Journal Watch 23(1); McCowan arXiv:2510.01484 (two-spillover reanalysis)
WHO SAGO report (2025); ODNI declassified assessment; Gelman (2025) (unresolved)
Worobey et al. (2022), Science 377(6609):951-959 (Huanan market epicenter)
Shin Jie Yong (2024), Medium, 'My Friend Won the US$100,000 Debate' (Miller's priors)
